prompt.name: Weather Card
prompt.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
prompt.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

initialMessages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups

prompt.name: Weather Card
prompt.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
prompt.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import { Card, Stack, Row, Text, Box } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface ForecastDay {
  dayName: string;
  condition: string;
  icon: string;
  highTemp: string;
  lowTemp: string;
}

interface Props {
  location?: string;
  currentTemp: string;
  condition: string;
  conditionIcon: string;
  humidity: string;
  windSpeed: string;
  forecast: ForecastDay[];
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  location = 'Current Location',
  currentTemp,
  condition,
  conditionIcon,
  humidity,
  windSpeed,
  forecast,
}: Props) {
  return (
    <Card
      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
      shadow="md"
      border
    >
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Stack gap={6}>
          <Text
            variant="caption"
            color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500, #6b7280)"
          >
            {location}
          </Text>
          <Row align="center" gap={8}>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)"
            >
              {conditionIcon}
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-700, #374151)"
            >
              {condition}
            </Text>
          </Row>
          <Text
            variant="h1"
            color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)"
            weight="bold"
          >
            {currentTemp}
          </Text>
        </Stack>

        <Row justify="between" gap={16}>
          <Box
            padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
            background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
            borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
          >
            <Stack gap={2}>
              <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500, #6b7280)">
                Humidity
              </Text>
              <Text variant="body" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)" weight="semibold">
                {humidity}
              </Text>
            </Stack>
          </Box>

          <Box
            padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
            background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
            borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
          >
            <Stack gap={2}>
              <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500, #6b7280)">
                Wind
              </Text>
              <Text variant="body" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)" weight="semibold">
                {windSpeed}
              </Text>
            </Stack>
          </Box>
        </Row>

        <Box
          padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
          background="var(--ggui-color-neutral-50, #f9fafb)"
          borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
        >
          <Row gap={12} wrap justify="between">
            {forecast.slice(0, 5).map((day) => (
              <Box
                key={day.dayName}
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm, 4px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
              >
                <Stack align="center" gap={4}>
                  <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-700, #374151)">
                    {day.dayName}
                  </Text>
                  <Text variant="body" color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)">
                    {day.icon}
                  </Text>
                  <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)" weight="semibold">
                    {day.highTemp}
                  </Text>
                  <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500, #6b7280)">
                    {day.lowTemp}
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>
            ))}
          </Row>
        </Box>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

initialMessages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
